frameworks.name: pattern completion
frameworks.one_line: recurrent local circuits fill in missing sensory features from partial input

frameworks.name: sensory inference
frameworks.one_line: brain combines prior expectations with incomplete input to infer perceptual objects

frameworks.name: top-down predictive signaling
frameworks.one_line: specialized V1 neurons receive and locally broadcast top-down information

core_question: What are the neural codes and circuits in V1 that implement sensory inference from incomplete visual input?
key_assumptions: perception of incomplete stimuli relies on prior expectations
illusory contours probe the neural substrate of sensory inference
holographic photoactivation of selected neurons can recreate a visual representation
manipulations: illusory bar stimuli vs component image segments
selective holographic optogenetic photoactivation of IC-encoder neurons
multi-Neuropixels and mesoscale 2p calcium imaging in mouse V1
key_findings: a specialized subset of V1 neurons responds emergently to illusory bars but not to component segments
selective photoactivation of these IC-encoders recreates the IC representation in V1 without any visual stimulus
interpretations: IC-encoder neurons are specialized to receive and locally broadcast top-down information
pattern completion circuits in lower cortical areas selectively reinforce activity matching prior expectations as a step in perceptual inference
